displayTitle: Block by Block: Calibrate by Comparison

beats.narration: An echo is just a voltage. Ultrasonic testing has no absolute scale: every reading is a comparison against known reflectors in known blocks.

payload.title: NO ABSOLUTE SCALE
payload.sub: Standardization by comparison with reference blocks.

beats.narration: First, range: the screen reads true distance in the part. Then sensitivity: peak a known reflector to eighty percent, and record that gain.

payload.heading: Two numbers every job starts with

chips.t: Range: true distance in the part

beats.narration: Peak the same size hole at quarter, half, and three-quarter thickness, gain untouched. Connect the marks: a DAC, judging indications at their own depth.

payload.heading: Build the DAC from three depths
payload.note: JUDGE EACH INDICATION AT ITS OWN DEPTH

beats.narration: A standardization holds while the system holds: same settings, same probe and cable by serial number, same couplant, temperature near the block's.
An echo means nothing until compared. The block is your scale, acoustically like the part, matched to the planned test.

payload.sub: The block is your scale — matched to part and test.
